title: Least Privilege for Containers
description:
## Reducing Blast Radius

A container only needs to read its application files and serve HTTP. Which combination best applies the principle of **least privilege**?

options.text: Run as root but on a user-defined network
Run as a non-root user, with a read-only root filesystem, and drop all Linux capabilities it doesn't need
Run with `--privileged` so it never hits a permission error
Add `--cap-add=ALL` to avoid debugging capability issues

explanation: Least privilege means giving the container only what it needs: a **non-root** user (`USER` in the Dockerfile or `--user`), a **read-only** root filesystem (`--read-only`, with `--tmpfs` for any writable scratch space), and **dropping capabilities** (`--cap-drop=ALL` then add back only the few required). `--privileged` and `--cap-add=ALL` do the exact opposite — they hand the container near-host-level power and massively increase the blast radius if it is compromised.
